name: agentifyme
documentation:
# AgentifyMe
Framework for agentic workflows






### Tests

```bash
rye run coverage run -m pytest

# For a simple terminal report
rye run coverage report

# For an HTML report
rye run coverage html
```

methods.name: agentifyme.document_stores.vector.base.VectorDocumentStore.list_collections
documentation:
List all collections in the document store.

Returns:
    A list of collection names.

methods.name: agentifyme.document_stores.vector.base.VectorDocumentStore.get_collection
documentation:
Get a collection from the document store.

Args:
    collection_name: The name of the collection to retrieve.

Returns:
    The collection.

methods.name: agentifyme.document_stores.vector.base.VectorDocumentStore.get_document
documentation:
Get a document from the document store.

Args:
    collection_name: The name of the collection to retrieve the document from.
    document_id: The ID of the document to retrieve.

Returns:
    The document.

methods.name: agentifyme.document_stores.vector.base.VectorDocumentStore.get_documents
documentation:
Get all documents from a collection.

Args:
    collection_name: The name of the collection to retrieve the documents from.

Returns:
    A list of documents.

methods.name: agentifyme.document_stores.vector.base.VectorDocumentStore.add_document
documentation:
Add a document to a collection.

Args:
    collection_name: The name of the collection to add the document to.
    document: The document to add.

Returns:
    None

documentation:

Returns:

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.time.timedelta_to_cron
documentation:
Convert a timedelta object to an approximate cron expression.

Args:
    td (timedelta): The timedelta object to convert.

Returns:
    str: An approximate cron expression.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the timedelta cannot be converted to a simple cron expression.

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.json_utils.extract_json
documentation:
Extracts the first JSON object from the given text, handling Markdown-style code blocks,
single-quoted JSON, and escaped quotes.

Args:

Returns:
    dict: The parsed JSON object, or None if no JSON object is found.

functions.name: agentifyme.cache.cache_factory
documentation:
Factory function to create a cache based on the given cache_type.

Args:
    cache_type (CacheType): The type of cache to create.

Returns:
    Cache[T]: An instance of the cache based on the cache_type.

functions.name: agentifyme.cache.cache
documentation:
Decorator that caches the result of a function based on its arguments.

Args:
    cache_type (CacheType, optional): The type of cache to use. Defaults to CacheType.NONE.

Returns:
    Callable[[F], F]: The decorated function.

classes.name: agentifyme.cache.CacheType
documentation: Cache types.

classes.name: agentifyme.cache.Cache

methods.name: agentifyme.cache.Cache.get
documentation:
Retrieve a value from the cache.

Args:
    key: The key associated with the value to retrieve.

Returns:
    The value associated with the given key, or None if the key is not found.

methods.name: agentifyme.cache.Cache.set
documentation:
Set a value in the cache.

Args:
    key: The key to associate with the value.
    value: The value to store in the cache.

Returns:
    None

Abstract base class for cache implementations.

Args:
    T: The type of values stored in the cache.

Attributes:
    None

Methods:
    get: Retrieve a value from the cache.
    set: Set a value in the cache.

classes.name: agentifyme.cache.MemoryCache

methods.name: agentifyme.cache.MemoryCache.get
documentation:
Get the value associated with the given key.

Args:
    key (str): The key to retrieve the value for.

Returns:
    Optional[T]: The value associated with the key, or None if the key is not found.

methods.name: agentifyme.cache.MemoryCache.set
documentation:
Set the value for the given key.

Args:
    key (str): The key to set the value for.
    value (T): The value to be stored.

Returns:
    None

A simple in-memory cache implementation.

This cache stores key-value pairs in memory and provides methods to get and set values.

Attributes:
    cache (Dict[str, T]): The dictionary that holds the cached values.

classes.name: agentifyme.cache.DiskCache

methods.name: agentifyme.cache.DiskCache.get
documentation:
Retrieve the value associated with the given key from the cache.

Args:
    key (str): The key used to identify the value in the cache.

Returns:
    Optional[T]: The value associated with the key, or None if the key does not exist in the cache.

methods.name: agentifyme.cache.DiskCache.set
documentation:
Store the given value in the cache with the specified key.

Args:
    key (str): The key used to identify the value in the cache.
    value (T): The value to be stored in the cache.

A disk-based cache implementation that stores objects using joblib.

Args:
    cache_dir (str, optional): The directory path where the cache files will be stored.
        Defaults to "/tmp/agentifyme-cache".

Attributes:
    cache_dir (str): The directory path where the cache files are stored.

classes.name: agentifyme.cache.NoCache
documentation:
A cache implementation that does not store any values.

This cache implementation always returns `None` when `get` is called,
and does nothing when `set` is called.

Attributes:
    None

Methods:
    get(key: str) -> Optional[T]: Returns `None` for any given key.
    set(key: str, value: T) -> None: Does nothing.

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.meta.get_function_metadata_with_ast
documentation:
Get metadata for a function.

Args:
    func (Callable): The function to analyze.

Returns:
    Tuple[List[str], List[str], str]: A tuple containing:
        - List of argument names
        - List of argument type annotations (or "Any" if not specified)
        - Return type annotation (or "Any" if not specified)

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.meta.function_metadata
documentation: Get metadata for a function.

classes.name: agentifyme.utilities.meta.Param
documentation:
Represents a parameter.

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the parameter.
    description (str): The description of the parameter.
    data_type (str): The data type of the parameter.
    default_value (Any): The default value of the parameter. Defaults to None.
    required (bool): Whether the parameter is required. Defaults to True.

classes.name: agentifyme.utilities.meta.FunctionMetadata
documentation:
Represents metadata for a function.

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the function.
    description (str): The description of the function.
    input_params (List[Param]): The input parameters of the function.
    output_params (List[Param]): The output parameters of the function.
    doc_string (str): The docstring of the function.

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.func_utils.get_function_metadata
documentation: Get metadata for a function.

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.func_utils.convert_json_to_args
documentation:
Convert JSON data to function arguments based on function signature and type hints.

Args:
    func (callable): The function to convert arguments for.
    json_data (Dict[str, Any]): The JSON data to convert.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: A dictionary of converted arguments.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the JSON data is invalid or doesn't match the function signature.

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.func_utils.validate_and_call_workflow
documentation:
Validate the JSON data against the workflow function's metadata and call the function.

Args:
    workflow_func (Callable): The workflow function to be called.
    json_data (Dict[str, Any]): The JSON data to be used as arguments.

Returns:
    Any: The result of the workflow function.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the JSON data is invalid or doesn't match the function signature.

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.func_utils.execute_function_sync
documentation:
Executes the given function with the provided JSON data as arguments.

Args:
    func (Callable): The function to be executed.
    json_data (Dict[str, Any]): The JSON data containing the arguments for the function.

Raises:
    ValueError: If a required parameter is missing or if the type of a parameter is invalid.

Returns:
    Any: The result of the function execution.

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.func_utils.execute_function_async
documentation:
Executes the given function with the provided JSON data as arguments.

Args:
    func (Callable): The function to be executed.
    json_data (Dict[str, Any]): The JSON data containing the arguments for the function.

Raises:
    ValueError: If a required parameter is missing or if the type of a parameter is invalid.

Returns:
    Any: The result of the function execution.

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.func_utils.execute_function
documentation:
Executes the given function with the provided JSON data as arguments.

Args:
    func (Callable): The function to be executed.
    json_data (Dict[str, Any]): The JSON data containing the arguments for the function.

Raises:
    ValueError: If a required parameter is missing or if the type of a parameter is invalid.

Returns:
    Any: The result of the function execution.

functions.name: agentifyme.utilities.func_utils.timedelta_to_cron
documentation:
Convert a timedelta object to a cron expression.
Returns a cron string in format: minute hour day month dow
Only handles periods up to 1 month.

methods.name: agentifyme.worker.telemetry.otel_handler.OTELHandler._str_to_log_level
documentation:
Convert a string representation of a logging level to its corresponding integer value.

```
param level_str

A string representing the logging level (case-insensitive)

return

The corresponding integer logging level

raises ValueError

If the input string doesn't match a valid logging level
```

methods.name: agentifyme.worker.telemetry.instrumentor.InstrumentationWrapper.get_attributes
documentation: # Create a meter
meter = metrics.get_meter("agentifyme")
# Create some metrics
workflow_duration = meter.create_histogram(
name="workflow_duration",
description="Duration of workflow execution",
unit="s",
task_duration = meter.create_histogram(
name="task_duration",
description="Duration of task execution",
unit="s",
workflow_counter = meter.create_counter(
name="workflows_executed",
description="Number of workflows executed",
task_counter = meter.create_counter(
name="tasks_executed",
description="Number of tasks executed",
fn_call_counter = meter.create_counter(
name="fn.total.count", description="Number of function calls"
fn_error_counter = meter.create_counter(
name="fn.errors.count",
description="Number of function call errors",

classes.name: agentifyme.config.BaseModule
documentation: Base class for modules in the agentifyme framework.

classes.name: agentifyme.config.TaskConfig
documentation:
Represents the configuration for a task.

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the task.
    description (str): The description of the task.
    objective (Optional[str]): The objective of the task.
    instructions (Optional[str]): The instructions for completing the task.
    tools (Optional[list[Tool]]): The list of tools required for the task.
    input_params (list[Param]): The list of input parameters for the task.
    output_params (list[Param]): The list of output parameters for the task.

classes.name: agentifyme.config.WorkflowConfig
documentation:
Represents a workflow.

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the workflow.
    slug (str): The slug of the workflow.
    description (Optional[str]): The description of the workflow (optional).
    func (Callable[..., Any]): The function associated with the workflow.
    input_parameters (dict[str, Param]): A dictionary of input parameters for the workflow.
    output_parameters (list[Param]): The list of output parameters for the workflow.
    schedule (Optional[Union[str, timedelta]]): The schedule for the workflow.
        Can be either a cron expression string or a timedelta object.
